text.title: Authentication Security Rules and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Token binding, rotation discipline, required negative tests, and common agent mistakes in auth code.

rule.statement: For browser clients, bind tokens as HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax cookies. Never store tokens in localStorage or sessionStorage.
rule.rationale: localStorage is accessible to any JavaScript running on the page; XSS attacks steal tokens from localStorage in one line. HttpOnly cookies are inaccessible to JavaScript.

section.title: Required tests for every auth change

rule.statement: Every auth code change must include negative tests: expired token rejected, wrong audience rejected, missing scope rejected, alg:none rejected.
rule.rationale: Auth bugs manifest as silent bypasses, not as exceptions. Positive tests pass with missing aud/iss checks; only negative tests catch those vulnerabilities.

rule.statement: Maintain a two-active-key window for secret/key rotation: the new key is added before the old is removed, so in-flight tokens continue to validate during the overlap.
rule.rationale: Single-key rotation invalidates all issued tokens simultaneously, causing a thundering herd of re-authentication requests from all active users.

rule.statement: Rate-limit /login and /oauth/token per IP and per account with exponential backoff on failure. Alert on credential-stuffing patterns (many failures, varied usernames, narrow IP range).
rule.rationale: Unthrottled auth endpoints enable credential stuffing and brute-force attacks at scale with no server-side defense.

antipattern.description: Agent generates a JWT secret inline in code or config files.
antipattern.reason: Secrets in source code are permanently exposed via git history. Use environment variables or a secrets manager (1Password CLI, HashiCorp Vault).

antipattern.description: Auth middleware catches JWTError and returns 200 with a default user.
antipattern.reason: Silent auth bypass — a bug in token validation becomes a security hole. Always return 401 on JWTError.

antipattern.description: Custom JWT validation logic instead of a library (authlib, jose, python-jose).
antipattern.reason: Custom crypto has a long history of subtle bugs: timing attacks, algorithm confusion, missing claim validation. Use a well-audited library.

text.reference: CI smoke test: fails if API accepts alg:none or wrong-aud tokens
